commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e76e9189f3b699a3cddb842896cc9ae5cad17736)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds routed thread metadata to reply operations, blocks steer mode when active and current routed thread ids differ, and extends Slack DM session-key and auto-reply regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main's steer decision ignores routed thread ids while Slack DMs keep one stable SessionKey and distinct MessageThreadId values. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human follow-up is needed because the PR has a blocking code gap and the external contributor still needs to supply real Slack behavior proof.

target.title: fix(slack): keep DM thread turns out of active steering
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve OpenClaw's existing stable Slack DM `SessionKey` contract for top-level DMs and ordinary DM thread replies.
- Track the routed thread id on active reply operations, then suppress steering when a new Slack DM turn has the same session key but a different Slack thread id.
- Add regression coverage proving a second top-level Slack DM from the same user is queued as its own follow-up turn instead of steering into the active run for the first DM thread.

## Verification

- Confirmed current `origin/main` already preserves stable top-level DM sessions and exposes the Slack message ts as `MessageThreadId`.
- Watched the new regression fail before the fix: `expected true to be false` for `call.shouldSteer`, proving the second Slack DM thread would still steer into the active run.
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Two overlapping top-level Slack DMs from the same user should keep the stable DM `SessionKey`, but the second DM thread must not steer into the active run for the first DM thread. It should be treated as a follow-up for its own routed Slack thread id.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at `e76e9189f3b699a3cddb842896cc9ae5cad17736` on macOS, using the repo's `pnpm` test runner against the real OpenClaw auto-reply runtime path and Slack prepare path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: I exercised the same local runtime path used after Slack prepare: an active reply operation for stable DM session `session-key` with routed Slack thread `500.000`, followed by a second Slack direct-message turn on the same `SessionKey` with `MessageThreadId=501.000`.
- Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw runtime run after this patch:

```text
shouldSteer = false
shouldFollowup = true
isActive = true
isStreaming = false
originatingThreadId = 501.000

$ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  70 passed (70)

$ pnpm test extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  165 passed (165)
```

- Observed result after fix: Stable Slack DM routing remains intact (`agent:main:slack:direct:u3` for both top-level DMs), while the active-run decision for the second DM thread reports `shouldSteer=false` and keeps `originatingThreadId=501.000`, so it will not append into the first DM thread's active turn.
- What was not tested: I did not include a recorded external Slack workspace run in this PR body. The proof above is copied local OpenClaw runtime output plus Slack prepare-path output from this checkout; live Slack workspace proof can still be added by a maintainer/Mantis run if required.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the fix, the same local runtime scenario failed with `AssertionError: expected true to be false` at `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts:1306`.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Slack DMs intentionally share the stable direct-message session key, but the active-run steering decision only compared session identity. A second top-level DM from the same user could therefore steer into the active run for the first Slack message thread.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing Slack prepare tests covered the stable DM session contract, but auto-reply routing tests did not cover same-session, different-thread active runs.
- Contributing context: The fix belongs at the active-run routing boundary, not in Slack session-key generation, because Slack DM session continuity is a shipped contract.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this: Unit test plus Slack prepare-path contract test.
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts` and `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Same stable Slack DM session, different routed Slack thread id, active prior run, second turn must not steer into the first run.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It exercises the auto-reply decision that caused the overlap while separately preserving the Slack session-key contract.
target.author: guanbear
